title: spt-core runtime manifest
description: Per-adapter runtime manifest for the spt-core harness contract. Authored as TOML (this schema describes the equivalent data model). A manifest declares only what varies per harness/shell; command templates are opaque strings spt-core never parses. Cross-field invariants (kind<->[shell] agreement, strategy/avenue field requirements) are enforced by spt-core's validate step beyond this schema.

hooks.description: `[hooks.<event>]` — inbound hook table, keyed by harness event name.

message-idle-translation-binary.description:
`[message-idle-translation-binary]` — opt-in adapter idle-delivery
translation binary (ADR-0022 / REQ-MSG-IDLE-TRANSLATION-BINARY). A TABLE
carrying a `path` scalar (modeled as a table, not a bare top-level scalar, so
an author who writes it after another section cannot have it silently
absorbed — and so it stays N+1 extensible). The binary is a pure
stdin→stdout JSON-lines filter: spt-core feeds it `init`/`event`/`input`
lines and reads back `{key}`/`{delay_ms}`/`{text}` keystroke-commands, which
spt-core applies to the broker-held PTY atomically (spt-core owns every PTY
write). spt-core LIFECYCLE-manages it (spawn when the spt-hosted endpoint
comes up, terminate when it goes down). A NEW manifest primitive — NOT
collapsed into `[inject]`/`notif_command` — though it shares the poll-feed
substrate. Absent ⇒ no translation binary (idle inbound SPOOLS, poll-fed;
the v0.11.0 raw-inject was removed as a delivery path, ADR-0022 amendment).

profiles.description:
`[profiles.<name>]` — **shipped** profile overlays: sparse leaf-replace
overlays declared by the adapter dev inside the parent manifest, updating
as one unit with it. Stored raw ([`toml::Value`]); [`crate::profile::resolve`]
merges one onto the base and re-validates the complete manifest. A bare
`adapter_name` ignores these (parent unmodified); the composite
`<adapter>:<profile>` selects one. **Local** (node-local, user-authored)
profiles live beside the adapter in the registry, never here.
(CONTEXT.md §adapter profile.)

strings.description:
`[strings]` — an adapter-authored KV tree of **opaque data** (spt-core
never executes a string; command templates live in their own sections
behind registration). Dot-path-readable via `spt adapter get-string`, and
it rides the same leaf-replace profile overlay as the rest of the manifest
(a shipped or local profile may override base strings). Node-local; no
cross-node sync. (CONTEXT.md §adapter strings.)

hints.description:
`[[hints]]` — once-per-session keyword hints (CONTEXT.md §keyword hints).
**Order is significant** (first match wins). A profile overlays this by
leaf-replace like any section — the array is replaced wholesale, never
spliced (override/extend = re-declare).

Adapter.description: `[adapter]` — the manifest header, readable before any update (compat gate).

min_spt_core_version.description: Lowest spt-core version this adapter tolerates (compat gate).

hostable_types.description: Endpoint types this adapter can host (`LiveAgent`, `Worker`, …).

shortcut_basename.description:
Optional basename for the `spt endpoint run` picker's `<basename>-<id>`
launcher shortcut (REQ-MANIFEST-7). Absent ⇒ the harness-agnostic default
`spt` (→ `spt-<id>`); an adapter sets this to brand its shortcuts
(spt-claude-code → `cc`, giving `cc-<id>`). Additive + N-1-safe (omitted
from serialization when absent). The picker reads it from the RESOLVED
manifest of the selected adapter.

host_binaries.description:
The harness executable basenames this `kind="harness"` adapter hosts
agents inside (e.g. `host_binaries = ["claude"]`). The bind-time
adapter-resolution match-key (REQ-MANIFEST-8 / ADR-0021): a harness
session's parent pid → its exe basename selects the candidate adapters
whose `host_binaries` contains it (case-insensitive, `.exe`-stripped), so
`listen`/`poll` resolve the owning adapter at bind with no mandatory
`--adapter`. Additive + N-1-safe (omitted from serialization when empty,
like `shortcut_basename`); an empty list is harmless (the adapter is never
a bind-time candidate, only reachable via the explicit `--adapter`).

AdapterKind.description:
The two adapter kinds. A `harness` hosts agents; a `shell` provides a driven
surface (MANIFEST §Shell adapters).

Hook.description:
`[hooks.<event>]` — one harness event → the `api` command it fires, the
stdin fields it maps, and whether it can surface context to the agent.

fires.description: Opaque `api …` command line the harness invokes for this event.

can_inject.description: Whether this hook can inject context (false ⇒ sentinel/relay fallback).

Session.description:
`[session]` — the watched-dir keys (`commune_dir`/`signoff_dir`) co-located
with the fixed set of `[session.<role>]` command templates.

resume.description:
`[session.resume]` — the agent's OWN-session NATIVE resume (the `self_`
sibling, mirroring `psyche_init`→`psyche_resume`). Selected over `self_`
only when a bringup carries `--resume <session>` AND this role is declared;
absent ⇒ fall back to `[session.self]` (full back-compat). Keys spt-core
fills are the SAME catalog as `self`: `{id}`, `{session_id}` (the resumed
id), `{session_name}`, `{adapter_name}` (REQ-SESSION-RESUME-TEMPLATE).

notif.description:
`[session.notif]` — the endpoint-native notification render
(ADR-0007's `notif_command` seam, REQ-NOTIF-2): an OS toast, a
GameRobot `alert-symbol`, anything the adapter can run. Spawned
detached when a notif surfaces at this endpoint, combinable with the
agent-surface delivery. Keys spt-core fills: `{notif_id}`,
`{notif_from}`, `{notif_subnet}`, `{notif_body}`.

SessionRole.description:
`[session.<role>]` — one opaque outbound command template plus its spawn
context. Model/tools/flags all live inside `command`, never as fields
(MANIFEST §session roles). No nested tables here (keeps TOML round-trip
emission scalar-before-table clean).

command.description: Opaque command line, with `{key}` substitution placeholders.

recursion_guard_env.description:
Env var set on summarizer children so their own hooks bail (recursion
guard).

env_remove.description: Env vars to strip from the child's inherited environment.

keys.description: Substitution keys spt-core guarantees to fill for this role.

value.description: Value to inject (with substitution); required for `inject`.

channel.description: Harness-hosted injection channel (spt-hosted inherits from the broker).

fetcher.description: `fetcher` strategy: adapter binary emitting normalized history.

locate_template.description: `locate_normalize` strategy: where the raw transcript lives.

normalize_command.description: `locate_normalize` strategy: command normalizing the raw transcript.

oneOf.description: spt-core asks the adapter (pull, adapter binary emits normalized).
spt-core locates the raw transcript then normalizes it.
Adapter pushes via `api history-log`; spt-core stores (Path-B).

Digest.description:
`[digest]` — the session-digest extractor seam (ADR-0019). Reverses M9's
"no manifest seam" stance: the digest gets its **own** adapter-declared
extractor, distinct from `[history]` (which stays opaque + single-session and
feeds the echo-commune verbatim). The extractor maps the harness's **native**
log → the published `{role, text, tool, ts}` digest-record contract
([`spt_term::DigestRecord`]).

**Imperative, not a DSL** (ADR-0019 §Decision): real harness logs are nested
(one line → many entries, mixed block lists, types to filter) — a flat
declarative map cannot express them, and a map powerful enough is a reinvented
language. So the extractor is an opaque command spt-core never parses, exactly
like every other manifest template.

**Source.** By default the extractor reads the **same files as `[history]`**
(the `locate_template`; DRY). An adapter may override with `source` (the
own-source escape hatch). `api digest-entry` push remains the always-available
fallback for a log-less adapter (which declares no `[digest]` at all).

**Presentation.** `window_turns`, `arg_truncation`, and `sprint_collapse` are
adapter-declared **defaults** any consumer may override at pull/subscribe;
spt-core ships fallback defaults ([`spt_term::DigestConfig`]) when absent. The
fixed "~3 turns" is no longer an spt-core requirement (ADR-0019).

strategy.description:
Which side locates the transcript (REQ-DIGEST-FETCHER-STRATEGY). Absent ⇒
`locate_normalize` (spt-core locates + pre-reads `source`) — the original
behavior, so every pre-strategy manifest parses unchanged.

extractor.description:
Opaque extractor command: native harness log → the `{role,text,tool,ts}`
contract (one JSON record per output line). `{key}` substitution applies
(`{session_id}`, the perch-bound `{cwd}`, captured `[env] direction="read"`
vars, and — in `locate_normalize` mode — `{source}` for the resolved path).

source.description:
Own-source escape hatch (`locate_normalize` only): a `locate_template` for
the log file the extractor reads. Absent ⇒ reuse `[history].locate_template`
(DRY). Ignored under `fetcher` (the extractor locates itself).

window_turns.description: Adapter-default window depth (user turns kept). Absent ⇒ spt-core fallback.

arg_truncation.description: Adapter-default tool-arg truncation width. Absent ⇒ spt-core fallback.

sprint_collapse.description:
Adapter-default for collapsing consecutive tool records into one sprint.
Absent ⇒ spt-core fallback (collapse on).

DigestStrategy.description:
`[digest]` locate-ownership strategy (REQ-DIGEST-FETCHER-STRATEGY) — mirrors
[`HistoryStrategy`]'s locate/normalize split, applied to the digest extractor.
Which side LOCATES the transcript:
- `locate_normalize` (default, back-compat): **spt-core** resolves the single
  `source` template to one file, reads it, and pipes the bytes to the extractor
  on stdin. Correct for a trivial single-file, fully-templatable layout.
- `fetcher`: the **adapter's** extractor locates + reads + emits normalized
  records itself; spt-core only runs it bounded and consumes stdout (no locate,
  no pre-read). Required for a PARTITIONED layout spt-core cannot express as one
  template — e.g. Claude Code's `projects/<munge(cwd)>/<session_id>.jsonl` or a
  date-globbed rollout tree — the CONTEXT §history "the adapter owns the locate"
  case. spt-core feeds the extractor only the harness-NEUTRAL inputs it owns
  (`{session_id}`, the perch-bound `{cwd}`, and captured `[env] direction="read"`
  vars), never a harness-specific project slug.

oneOf.description:
spt-core locates the single `source` file and pipes its bytes to the
extractor (the original ADR-0019 behavior — the default for back-compat).

The adapter's extractor locates + reads + emits; spt-core runs it bounded
and consumes stdout (no `source`, no pre-read).

IdleTranslationBinary.description:
`[message-idle-translation-binary]` — the opt-in idle-delivery translation
binary (ADR-0022). A table so the contract degrades gracefully: spt-core does
NOT `deny_unknown_fields`, so a newer adapter declaring a future key (e.g. a
spawn timeout) against an older spt-core parses fine — the unknown key is
ignored, never a hard manifest failure (a lifecycle-binary contract perri
builds blind from docs must be forward-compatible). Known keys: `command`
(preferred) and the deprecated `path`.

command.description:
The opaque command spt-core spawns and lifecycle-manages (ADR-0029):
program + args, with `{adapter_dir}`/`{adapter_name}` substitution; the
program token resolves against `install_dir` like `[digest].extractor` /
`[session.psyche_init]`. The spawn + stdin/stdout JSON-lines protocol is
unchanged — `command` only alters how the executable+args are located.
Folds `claude-spt translate` into the one consolidated adapter binary.

path.description:
**Deprecated** (ADR-0029): the bare binary PATH spt-core spawns. Keeps
parsing (manifest forward/back-compat) but warns at registration steering
to `command`. Resolved against `install_dir`; a single program token (no
substitution, never re-tokenized). Exactly one of `{command, path}` —
both-set is refused at validation; neither = no translation binary.

Identity.description: `[identity]` — how the harness's session id is obtained.

parent_ancestor_name.description: Process-tree anchor name when `session_id` is absent.

oneOf.description: Injected as a UUID the harness echoes back.

Update.description: `[update]` — adapter self-update directive (parsed in M2a; conducted in M3).

command.description: `delegated` avenue: the command spt-core delegates to.

repo.description:
`file_pull` / `gh_release` avenue: source repo. For `gh_release` this is
the `user/repo` whose **GitHub releases** the adapter ships updates from.

asset.description:
`gh_release` avenue: the release **asset** name to fetch (the adapter
`.spt` archive). Absent ⇒ the default `adapter.spt`, matching the
`spt adapter add --release` acquisition primitive (REQ-INSTALL-9). Not
applicable to `delegated` / `file_pull`.

signing_key.description:
The adapter's Ed25519 **content-signing public key** (64 hex chars / 32
bytes). spt-core verifies a pulled payload against this per-adapter key
before applying it (REQ-UPD-5 adapter content signing, ADR-0004 §D) — the
adapter author signs their own releases; spt-core's release key stays
scoped to spt-core. **Required for `file_pull`** (there are bytes to
verify); **optional for `gh_release`** (absent ⇒ HTTPS+GitHub
first-acquisition trust, the same trust `spt adapter add --release` and
the installer first-fetch place; present ⇒ the fetched `.spt` is verified
fail-closed against this key, REQ-UPD-9); not applicable to `delegated`
(opaque updater).

self_verifies.description:
`delegated` avenue: the adapter attests its own updater verifies the
content it installs (e.g. `claude.exe plugin update` checks its own
signatures). spt-core cannot see a delegated updater's bytes, so it
delegates the trust **only** when this is set; an unattested delegated
update is skipped as unverifiable (REQ-UPD-5).

uninstall.description:
Optional inverse of install — run by `spt adapter remove` once the adapter
is quiesced (the mirror of `spt adapter add`, which reuses this section as
the install mechanism). Absent ⇒ spt-core's default cleanup. (Modeled in
M2a; conducted with adapter-registration later.)

message.description:
Optional plain (multi-line) human notice surfaced to stdout —
markdown-rendered (the helpfmt prose path) — **only when `spt adapter
update` actually APPLIES an update** (version changed), never on a
no-op. Read from the newly-installed manifest; avenue-agnostic
(`gh_release` / `delegated` / `file_pull`). No `{key}` substitution.
Use: an adapter telling the operator a post-update action (e.g. "run
`/reload-plugins` in any ongoing sessions").

post.description:
`[update.post]` — an **avenue-agnostic** delegated post-step (ADR-0029) run
AFTER the primary avenue resolves, in the same `spt adapter update`.
Absent ⇒ today's behavior exactly (an applied update fires `message`).

transport.description:
`gh_release` avenue: the fetch **transport** — `https` (direct reqwest,
public), `gh` (shell the pre-authorized `gh` CLI, the private-repo path),
or `auto` (default: prefer `gh` when installed+authed, else HTTPS). `gh`
honors OAuth + `GH_TOKEN`, so spt-core custodies no token. Additive over
the existing fetch path; not applicable to `delegated` / `file_pull`.
Absent ⇒ `auto` (N-1-safe).

oneOf.description: Delegate to the adapter's own updater (e.g. `claude plugin update`).
spt-core pulls files from a repo.

spt-core ships updates from the adapter's own **GitHub releases**
(REQ-UPD-9): compare the repo's latest release version against the
installed adapter version and, when newer, fetch the release `.spt`
archive (the REQ-INSTALL-9 `--release` primitive), verify it against an
optional `signing_key` (else HTTPS+GitHub trust), and re-register. No
signing tooling or plugin coupling required of the adapter author.

UpdatePost.description:
`[update.post]` — an avenue-agnostic delegated post-step (ADR-0029) run after
the primary `[update]` avenue (gh_release / file_pull / delegated) resolves,
in the same `spt adapter update`. Runs **unconditionally** — even when the
adapter pull was a version no-op — because the post-step does its own
idempotent check (e.g. `claude plugin update`). spt-core feeds it the update
outcome as one stdin JSON line (`adapter_applied`, `adapter_name`,
`profile_name`, `version`, `previous_version`, `adapter_dir`; additive keys)
and reads its stdout to arbitrate the post-update notice (custom text
supersedes `[update].message`; the reserved sentinel fires the static
`[update].message`; empty = no notice). exit code is orthogonal (0 ok /
nonzero failed). Failure-isolated: a committed `gh_release` pull is never
rolled back if the post-step fails. (A table so unknown future keys degrade
gracefully — no `deny_unknown_fields`.)

command.description:
The command spt-core runs after pull+re-register. Opaque; `{adapter_dir}`/
`{adapter_name}` substitution; the program token resolves against the
install dir (REQ-INSTALL-11). Validated non-empty.

self_verifies.description:
The post-step attests it verifies the content it installs (mirrors
`[update].self_verifies` for the delegated avenue — e.g. `claude plugin
update` checks its own signatures). Attestation metadata; the post-step
runs unconditionally regardless (the trust model around delegated content
the post-step installs, not an execution gate).

Transport.description:
Fetch transport for the `gh_release` avenue (and `spt adapter add --release`):
how spt-core retrieves the release asset bytes + the latest-release version.
(REQ-ADAPTER-GH-TRANSPORT)

oneOf.description: Direct HTTPS via reqwest — the public-repo path (the original behavior).

Shell the pre-authorized `gh` CLI (`gh release download` for the asset,
`gh api` for the version) — the private-repo path. `gh` honors OAuth +
`GH_TOKEN`, so spt-core never custodies a token.

Prefer `gh` when it is installed and authenticated, else fall back to
HTTPS. The default.

Shell.description: `[shell]` — the body of a `kind = "shell"` adapter (a driven surface).

ephemeral.description: Ephemeral ⇒ no offline perch + no history retention.

command_receipt.description: How the shell receives agent commands.

pre_close.description: Instruction sent to the binary on link-break.

persistent.description: Auto-online whenever the owner endpoint is online.

wake_command.description: Long-running wake-watcher run WHILE offline; exit ⇒ revive.

can_shutdown.description: Whether the shell may fire `api owner-shutdown` to suspend its owner.

require_approval.description:
Per-spawn user approval gate (floor; a node/endpoint setting may tighten).
Absent ⇒ `none`. (Modeled now; conducted when shells land.)

max_instances_per_owner.description:
Optional ceiling on concurrent-existing instances per owner endpoint
(online + offline both count). Absent ⇒ unlimited.

over_cap.description:
What happens at the cap: `reject` (default) or `approve` (per-spawn
approval beyond the cap; does not raise it). Only meaningful with a cap.

drive.description:
`[shell.drive]` — the drive vocabulary (agent→shell, REST-only ephemeral
control; M11-W2, REQ-SHELL-3). The owner→shell mirror of `[shell.sensory]`.

tunnel.description:
`[shell.tunnel]` — opt-in for the opaque reliable-ordered byte tunnel
(M11-W3, REQ-SHELL-4). Absent ⇒ no tunnel.

ShellApproval.description:
Per-shell instantiation-approval mode (`require_approval`). Reuses the consent
plumbing: `remembered` lets allow-always write a persistent grant; `always`
suppresses allow-always (prompt every spawn).

oneOf.description: No approval (default — matches the system's everything-opt-in posture).
Prompt; allow-always persists a grant, later spawns auto-allow.
Prompt on every spawn; allow-always suppressed (no persistent grant).

OverCap.description: What happens when an owner is at its `max_instances_per_owner` cap.

oneOf.description: Require per-spawn approval beyond the cap (does not raise the cap).

ShellCapability.description:
One entry in `[shell.capabilities]` — a command and its argument names.

A capability may carry its own **act-gate** (M11, REQ-CONSENT-3): the same
`require_approval` enum the spawn gate uses, riding the individual op so the
*dangerous act* is gated, not just the spawn. An optional `class_key` scopes
the written grant finer than the op id — granted per `(owner × class × node)`
(the usbip `attach`'s device class), so a remembered HID-class grant never
authorizes a storage-class act. Spawn gates govern existence; capability
gates govern acts (CONTEXT §"per-capability approval gates").

require_approval.description:
Per-act approval gate (floor; a node/endpoint setting may tighten).
Absent ⇒ `none` (ungated).

class_key.description:
Optional grant-qualifier class so a grant scopes finer than the op id
(`(owner × class × node)`). Only meaningful with a gated `require_approval`.

Sensory.description: `[shell.sensory]` — the sensory payload types a shell may emit.

Drive.description:
`[shell.drive]` — the drive payload types an agent may push to a shell
(M11-W2, REQ-SHELL-3). The owner→shell mirror of [`Sensory`]: REST-only,
ephemeral latest-wins, never spooled.

Tunnel.description:
`[shell.tunnel]` — the opt-in for the opaque reliable-ordered byte tunnel
(M11-W3, REQ-SHELL-4): a dedicated QUIC stream pair bound to the owner↔shell
link, carrying wire protocol traffic the channel taxonomy must NOT reinterpret
(first consumer: USB/IP URB traffic). Not enveloped, not MAC-framed, not
spooled; the link lifecycle governs it (a link-break closes the tunnel).
Reliable-ordered ⇒ congestion surfaces as lag never loss ⇒ on-LAN posture.

enable.description: Whether this shell opens the opaque tunnel on link-up.

protocol.description:
Optional diagnostic label for the opaque wire protocol (e.g. `usbip-urb`).
Informational only — the substrate never interprets tunnel bytes.

Hint.description:
`[[hints]]` — one once-per-session keyword hint (CONTEXT.md §keyword hints).
The adapter's user-prompt hook pipes the full user message to `spt api hint`;
a matching keyword surfaces `text` to the agent's context channel, at most
once per session and once per message.

keywords.description:
Keywords that fire the hint — literal **case-insensitive substrings** by
default; compiled as **regex** patterns when `regex = true`.

text.description: The hint text surfaced when a keyword matches.

regex.description: Treat `keywords` as regex patterns instead of literal substrings.
